<findings>

[suggestion] [warning] Zone "Prd Domain Operations" (packages-rex:prd-domain-operations) has critical risk (score: 0.64, cohesion: 0.36, coupling: 0.64) — requires refactoring before new feature development

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.36) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.64) — 63 imports target "prd-schema-foundation"
- 17 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- At 32 files this is the largest non-analysis zone; the breadth of concerns (delete, merge, reorganize, sync, analytics, requirements, keywords) suggests it is a catch-all that would benefit from sub-zone decomposition as the codebase grows.
- 'src/public.ts' as an entry point means external consumers of the rex package surface passes through this zone — keep public.ts lean and ensure it only re-exports stable, intentional API boundaries.
- The 63 imports into 'Prd Schema Foundation' and 20 imports into 'Prd Analysis Engine' indicate this zone is the primary integration point between schema, analysis, and executable operations.
- Coupling of 0.64 slightly exceeds the 0.6 warning threshold; with 32 files spanning analytics, sync, reorganization, and CRUD, the zone risks becoming a god-module — proactively identifying sub-clusters (e.g. sync+canonical, prune+delete+merge) would improve maintainability.
- 'src/store/contracts.ts' is classified as a store file but resides in a core-dominated zone; misplaced contracts can blur the boundary between the persistence interface and domain logic — verify it belongs here and not in the store zone.
- The bidirectional import relationship with 'Prd Cli Operations' (9 imports each direction) creates a potential circular dependency risk; enforce that core domain files never import from CLI command files.
- Zone "prd-domain-operations" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- src/public.ts — the package's external API surface — resides inside this 32-file catch-all zone. Any structural decomposition of prd-domain-operations must preserve public.ts exports without breakage, coupling zone-refactoring work to external API stability until public.ts is extracted to a stable, independent entry-point location.
- The 9 imports from prd-domain-operations into prd-cli-operations (core → unit-cli) are a confirmed architecture violation: domain logic must not import from CLI command files per the four-tier hierarchy. These reverse imports likely carry shared constants or output-formatting helpers that have drifted into the CLI layer.
- src/public.ts residing in a 32-file catch-all zone couples external API stability to internal zone health — extracting public.ts to a dedicated entry-point location before decomposing this zone would decouple these concerns.
- The 9 imports from prd-domain-operations (core) into prd-cli-operations (unit-cli) violate the layering rule that domain logic must not import from CLI commands. Shared types or constants driving these imports should be moved down to the schema/foundation layer.
- prd-domain-operations (32 files, cohesion 0.36, coupling 0.64) represents the highest absolute blast-radius risk in the package: it combines the largest file count with near-threshold values on both metrics. A single cross-cutting schema change would require coordinated updates across more files here than in any other zone, yet its near-threshold coupling has not been flagged with the same urgency as zones already above threshold.
- The zone contains files from 6 directories but has no barrel export to declare which of those directories' files constitute its public API. Without a per-zone barrel, the 17 entry points identified in the zone are all equally importable — there is no structural signal for which are internal implementation files versus intentional public exports.
- prd-domain-operations has the highest total blast-radius risk in the package (32 files × coupling 0.64 × cohesion 0.36). Even though its individual metrics are near but not at threshold, its file count means any threshold-crossing change will require the most simultaneous edits of any zone. Prioritize decomposition before the zone grows further.
- Add a src/core/public.ts barrel that explicitly declares which core utilities are part of the zone's public API. Without it, the 17 importable entry points across 6 directories give consumers no signal about which files are stable contracts versus internal implementation details.
- [call graph] 954 internal calls, 141 outgoing, 34 incoming (cohesion: 0.87, coupling: 0.13)

</insights>
